component_identification.content: Player pots/boards with scoring and ruby tracks, ingredient books/tables, draw bags, ingredient chips (white cherry bombs plus colored effects), flask tokens, rat tails, droplet markers, and fortune-teller/event cards. Each player builds a personalized bag over rounds.

core_game_loop.content: Each round, reveal event (if used), then players simultaneously draw chips from bags, placing them along pot spirals and deciding whether to stop or push luck. If white chip total exceeds 7, pot explodes. After drawing, players score, collect coins, buy new chips, and reset for next round.

detailed_rules.content: Start with a basic chip set in each bag. During brewing, place drawn chips in order; position determines points/coins value and triggers chip-specific effects based on current ingredient set. White chips are dangerous: sums of white values above 7 cause explosion, preventing either points or buying choice (edition-dependent you choose one). Players who stop safely may claim both scoring and buying benefits. Rubies gained from certain spaces can move droplet forward (improving future starts) or refill flask. Flask lets you return the last drawn white chip once per round before busting, then flips exhausted until refilled. Rat tails grant catch-up start spaces for players behind on score. End-phase includes bonus die for farthest safe brewer. Ingredient books have multiple rule sets; all players use the same set per color for a game.

scoring_and_endgame.content: After round nine (or final round by edition), players add track points, leftover rubies/coins conversions, and bonus points for non-exploded final brew. Highest total wins. Ties break by furthest pot position then shared victory as applicable.

beginner_strategy.content: Stop one draw earlier than your gut says in early rounds; consistent income beats flashy busts. Prioritize chips that improve draw control or economy, not only big movement. Use flask proactively when it preserves a strong round rather than hoarding it. Watch which ingredient sets are in play and pivot purchases accordingly. Rat-tail catch-up can be powerful—if behind, maximize safe scoring instead of desperate all-in pushes every round.
